message:
perf(matrix-rain): Add visibility detection and reduce FPS for better performance

The MatrixRain canvas animation was consuming CPU/GPU resources even when
not visible to the user (tab hidden, element scrolled offscreen). This
contributed to sluggish performance on desktop browsers.

Performance optimizations:

1. TAB VISIBILITY DETECTION
   - Listen to document.visibilitychange event
   - Stop animation loop when document.hidden is true
   - Resume animation when tab becomes visible again

2. INTERSECTION OBSERVER
   - Create IntersectionObserver with 0.01 threshold
   - Stop animation when canvas scrolls out of viewport
   - Resume when canvas becomes visible again
   - Graceful fallback if IntersectionObserver unavailable

3. FPS REDUCTION
   - Reduced target FPS from 30 to 24
   - Matrix rain effect is subtle enough that lower framerate is imperceptible
   - Reduces draw calls by 20%

4. ANIMATION LIFECYCLE
   - Refactored to use startAnimation/stopAnimation helpers
   - Proper cleanup: removes event listeners, disconnects observer
   - animationId tracked as null when stopped to prevent duplicate frames

The animation now only runs when:
- Component is mounted
- Tab is visible (not hidden)
- Canvas element is in viewport
- reducedMotion preference is false
- tuiMode is false

This eliminates unnecessary rendering when the effect isn't being viewed,
improving overall application responsiveness.
